_doc: Reliability manifest for the MCP fleet — consumed by `mcp-sync verify` and per-repo deploy/preflight.sh. For each server: whether a loop that lists it should treat it as REQUIRED (fail loud / exit non-zero at preflight) or optional (record degraded, continue); the credential it needs and where that cred lives; the token type; and the cheap auth probe to run at startup. Server *definitions* live in mcp.json; this file is the operational contract. See the deep-research report (2026-05-30) for rationale.

slack-dm.scope_source: x-oauth-scopes response header on any Web API call (NOT auth.test body)
slack-dm.preflight: auth.test -> ok:true AND assert required_scopes subset of x-oauth-scopes header
slack-dm.notes: Read path uses conversations.history (im:history) + inline reactions (reactions:read). NEVER search.messages (user-token/search:read only). Scope changes require an app REINSTALL (human). Reactions returned inline; reactions.get(full=true) only for the complete reactor list.

github.token_type: fine-grained PAT (ghp_/github_pat_) — NOT a gh CLI gho_ OAuth token, NOT a ghs_ App installation token
github.preflight: GET /rate_limit or get_me with the Bearer PAT -> 200
github.notes: gho_ failed with 401: the remote MCP validates token audience and a gh-CLI OAuth user token has the wrong audience. Remote endpoint accepts only (1) one-click OAuth or (2) a PAT. Headless/CI loops should OMIT this server and use the `gh` CLI (vizcom-sre, vizcom-review) instead of shipping a server that can't auth unattended.

datadog.preflight: any read with DD_API_KEY and DD_APPLICATION_KEY headers -> 200
datadog.notes: US5. Use ?toolsets= to limit exposed tools (already core,apm,error-tracking,software-delivery). VM env maps existing DD_APP_KEY to DD_APPLICATION_KEY for the official remote MCP header contract.

sentry.preflight: mcp-remote/whoami when ~/.mcp-auth is present; otherwise treat as optional/degraded.
sentry.notes: Official hosted Sentry MCP bridged through mcp-remote for all stdio clients. Auth once per host with `mcp-auth login sentry`; VM auth uses `mcp-auth vm-login sentry <host>`.

linear.preflight: mcp-remote OAuth/account probe when ~/.mcp-auth is present; otherwise treat as optional/degraded.
linear.notes: Official hosted Linear MCP bridged through mcp-remote for all stdio clients. Auth once per host with `mcp-auth login linear`; VM auth uses `mcp-auth vm-login linear <host>`.

notion.preflight: mcp-remote OAuth/account probe when ~/.mcp-auth is present; otherwise treat as optional/degraded.
notion.notes: Official hosted Notion MCP bridged through mcp-remote for all stdio clients. It acts with the connected user's Notion permissions. Auth once per host with `mcp-auth login notion`; VM auth uses `mcp-auth vm-login notion <host>`.

granola.preflight: mcp-remote/get_account_info when ~/.mcp-auth is present; otherwise treat as optional/degraded.
granola.notes: Official Granola MCP bridged through mcp-remote for all stdio clients. Browser OAuth only; no API key or service-account MCP auth. Auth once per host with `mcp-auth login granola`; VM auth uses `mcp-auth vm-login granola <host>`.

cloudflare.token_type: OAuth (interactive) or scoped Cloudflare API token
cloudflare.preflight: mcp-remote OAuth/account probe when ~/.mcp-auth is present; otherwise treat as optional/degraded.
cloudflare.notes: Official Cloudflare API MCP bridged through mcp-remote for all stdio clients. Auth once per host with `mcp-auth login cloudflare`; VM auth uses `mcp-auth vm-login cloudflare <host>`.

slack.token_type: Slack confidential OAuth via a directory-published or internal Slack app
slack.preflight: wrapper starts and mcp-remote OAuth succeeds when Slack app credentials and ~/.mcp-auth are present; otherwise treat as optional/degraded.
slack.notes: Official Slack MCP bridged through bin/slack-official-mcp because Slack does not support Dynamic Client Registration. Auth once per host with `mcp-auth login slack`; VM auth uses `mcp-auth vm-login slack <host>` after installing host-local Slack app credentials.

npx-stdio.notes: npx-launched stdio servers need node and a (possibly cold) network fetch on first run — keep them OUT of headless/VM strict configs (they contributed to the claude -p first-turn hang). Versions are pinned in mcp.json; run `mcp-sync drift` in CI.

preflight_policy.required_fail: exit non-zero with a named per-server/per-scope error so the runner alerts; do not start the tick.
preflight_policy.optional_fail: record a degraded-capability line in the tick output (never claim success for a down capability) and continue.
preflight_policy.strict_config_caveat: --strict-mcp-config does NOT override disabledMcpServers in ~/.claude.json (Claude Code bug #14490); verify must catch a required server disabled there. Always pass a non-empty --mcp-config with strict or it silently strips all servers.
